name: 2026-06-04 Political Commentary Senate Twelve For The Chair TH
description:
Twelve senators showed up yesterday to take the Senate chair.

That was enough to declare a quorum, install an acting Senate president, and adjourn the first regular session.

For days the same chamber could not find that twelfth seat to vote on a single bill for the country. Para sa pwesto, kaya. Para sa bayan, hindi.

The math only works when the seat is the prize.

custom_fields.value:
Twelve senators showed up yesterday to take the Senate chair.

That was enough to declare a quorum, install an acting Senate president, and adjourn the first regular session.

For days the same chamber could not find that twelfth seat to vote on a single bill for the country. Para sa pwesto, kaya. Para sa bayan, hindi.

The math only works when the seat is the prize.

Twelve senators showed up yesterday to take the Senate chair.

That was enough to declare a quorum, install an acting Senate president, and adjourn the first regular session.

For days the same chamber could not find that twelfth seat to vote on a single bill for the country. Para sa pwesto, kaya. Para sa bayan, hindi.

The math only works when the seat is the prize.

On 2026-06-03 the Senate declared a quorum with 12 senators (Avelino v. Cuenco doctrine + 2015 precedent; pool of 22 excluding one senator detained on plunder and one in ICC-warrant hiding). Sen. Sherwin Gatchalian was elected Senate President Pro Tempore and acting Senate President; the chamber then adopted a concurrent resolution adjourning the first regular session of the 20th Congress sine die. For days prior, the same chamber had failed to muster a working quorum to advance bills. Cayetano publicly refused to step down, calling it an 'illegal coup d'etat'. Malacanang recognized the new majority.
